I agree with the assessment provided. Chronic alcoholics indeed pose some level of risk related to weakened immunity, yet this risk doesn’t rise to the same degree as it does for individuals undergoing TNF-α inhibitor treatments, having silicosis, or being on hemodialysis—all of whom face heightened susceptibility to various opportunistic infections, including latent TB. Therefore, while chronic alcoholics could potentially develop active TB under certain circumstances, regular screening prior to initiating treatment is less stringent compared to the others listed.
